title: –end-of-options
excerpt: The git flag I assumed was an LLM hallucination
content_html:
I was reading through the fix for a package manager CVE last week and ran into a git flag I’d somehow never noticed: --end-of-options. My first reaction was that some LLM had hallucinated it, but it’s documented in gitcli(7), it was added in git 2.24.0 in November 2019, and it exists because git had already used -- for something else.

In most Unix tools -- marks the end of option parsing, so rm -- -f removes a file called -f rather than passing the force flag. Git had repurposed -- early on to separate revisions from pathspecs, because git log foo on its own is ambiguous between a branch named foo and a file named foo and one of the two readings needed a marker: git log main -- README.md means commits on main touching that file. That left the revision position with no terminator, so if a script runs git log "$rev" and $rev starts with a dash, git parses it as an option.

From the commit that introduced --end-of-options:

But that doesn’t work for the revision parser, because -- is already meaningful there: it separates revisions from pathspecs. So we need some other marker to separate options from revisions.

-- and --end-of-options are different things in git, and treating them as interchangeable is a mistake I’ve now seen in several places. Putting -- before a URL in git clone -- "$url" works, because clone follows the POSIX convention. A trailing -- after a ref, as in git checkout "$ref" --, marks $ref as a revision rather than a filename but still lets it be read as an option first. Passing an untrusted revision safely means writing git log --end-of-options "$rev" -- "$path", with both markers doing separate jobs.

Support for the new flag arrived per subcommand rather than all at once: git rev-parse only got it in 2.30.0, a year after the initial release, because it has its own hand-rolled argument parser, and git checkout and git reset rejected it until 2.43.1 in February 2024 because they parse -- themselves and the initial implementation left --end-of-options in the argument list where their parsers rejected it.

Argument injection

Git, hg, and ssh all ship options whose documented purpose is to run a command the caller names. git clone accepts --upload-pack=<cmd> to specify the server-side binary, and any git invocation accepts -c core.sshCommand=<cmd> to override how it connects. Mercurial accepts --config=alias.<subcmd>=!<shell> on any subcommand, which redefines the subcommand you’re running as an arbitrary shell script. ssh accepts -oProxyCommand=<cmd>. These are documented features that become attack primitives when a wrapping program passes an untrusted string into the argument list.

The failure mode has its own CWE, CWE-88, argument injection, and it’s distinct from command injection because there’s no shell involved: the wrapping program builds an argv array and calls exec directly, exactly as every “don’t use system()” guide recommends, the array reaches git intact, and git then parses one of the arguments as an option because it starts with a dash. CVE-2019-13139 in docker build is a clean example: Go’s os/exec package, an argv array, no shell, and a git-context URL whose #ref:dir fragment reached git fetch origin <ref> as --upload-pack=<cmd>.

The pattern was demonstrated across four version control systems on the same day in August 2017, when CVE-2017-1000117 (git), CVE-2017-1000116 (Mercurial), CVE-2017-9800 (Subversion), and CVE-2017-12836 (CVS) were disclosed together. Each passed a URL’s hostname to ssh as an argument, and a hostname starting with -oProxyCommand= became an ssh option. Phabricator’s post-mortem on the disclosure noted that of the three actively maintained tools, only Subversion actually added -- before the hostname in its fix; git and Mercurial validated the hostname format instead, partly because -- isn’t supported by every ssh implementation. The same write-up called the -- mechanism itself “unsafe by default”, since code without it looks correct and works fine right up until an argument starts with a dash.

Package managers

Package managers routinely take a git URL or ref as data and pass it to a subprocess: gem 'foo', git: '...' in a Gemfile, github:user/repo#ref in a package.json, and equivalents in pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, mix.exs, Package.swift, pubspec.yaml, conanfile.py, and go.mod. The URL and ref arrive in a manifest, a lockfile, or a transitive dependency’s metadata.

Of nineteen package managers I checked1, seventeen fork the git binary as their default or only path. The two that default to a library are Cargo, which uses libgit2 with an opt-in net.git-fetch-with-cli setting to fork instead, and Poetry, which switched to dulwich in 1.2.0 with a system-git-client setting to fall back. Nix uses libgit2 for reading local repositories but forks git for fetches, because libgit2 lacks git-credential helper support.

The published CVEs against package managers in this class include CVE-2021-43809 (Bundler), CVE-2021-29472 and CVE-2022-24828 (Composer), CVE-2022-36069 (Poetry), CVE-2023-5752 (pip), CVE-2022-21223 and CVE-2022-24440 (CocoaPods), and CVE-2025-68119 (Go). The Snyk research that produced several of the 2022 entries is written up here, and Sonar maintains a catalogue of the dangerous options per binary.

Of the seventeen that fork git, exactly one uses --end-of-options: Go’s cmd/go. It added -- before repository URLs in June 2019 as a general hardening pass. In January 2026 that turned out to be insufficient and --end-of-options was added across the board as the fix for CVE-2025-68119, along with HGPLAIN=+strictflags, which has restricted Mercurial’s early-option parsing since hg 4.4.2 in 2017. The commit message ends: “We should probably follow up with a more structured change to make it harder to accidentally re-introduce these issues in the future, but for now this addresses the issue at hand.”

Minimum git versions

The other package managers that guard the argument list at all use -- or a leading-dash check on the input, and looking at when each guard was added, most arrived as the fix for a reported vulnerability rather than in the original implementation. The -- before the URL in Bundler’s git clone is the CVE-2021-43809 patch. The leading-dash rejection in cocoapods-downloader landed in three commits over ten days in March 2022, matching the CVE-2022-21223 disclosure. Poetry’s guard arrived in September 2021 with a CVE assigned a year later, and the switch to dulwich followed six months after that. vcpkg is the exception I found where -- was present from the day git registry support was written.

Composer’s advisory for CVE-2022-24828 explains why almost none of these tools use --end-of-options: it names the flag as the correct fix and then says Composer supports git versions that predate it, so the patch rejects leading-dash branch names instead. vcpkg’s git integration has a comment stating a floor of git 2.7.4. Homebrew’s HOMEBREW_MINIMUM_GIT_VERSION is 2.7.0 on Linux, set in 2018.

Amazon Linux 2, which packaged git 2.14.3, reached end of life last month, so the distributions those floors track are only now ageing out. Ubuntu 18.04 with git 2.17.0 is in extended support until 2028. Ubuntu 20.04, in extended support until 2030, packages 2.25.1, which is new enough to accept --end-of-options on git fetch but old enough to reject it on git rev-parse. Relying on the flag means raising the minimum git to 2.24.0 for most subcommands, 2.30.0 for rev-parse, or 2.43.1 for checkout and reset, and losing anyone still on the distribution-packaged git.

Git libraries

libgit2, gitoxide, go-git, JGit, and dulwich all implement enough of the git wire protocol to clone and fetch in-process, with no argv boundary and so no argument list to inject into. Jujutsu uses gitoxide for its git interop and has no published CVEs in the argument-injection class; its two advisories to date are a path traversal and a missing SHA-1 collision check inherited from the library. go-git has one, CVE-2025-21613, and it’s specifically on the file:// transport, the one code path in go-git that spawns the git binary.

I noted in the package manager CWEs post that this trades one problem for another, since a bundled git implementation has to track every checkout-safety fix upstream git ships, and libgit2 and JGit have both had rounds of those. That’s a real cost, but it’s a stream of specific patches to apply rather than a check that has to be remembered at every call site forever.

Writing this prompted me to open a PR against Homebrew raising its minimum git to 2.30.0 and adding --end-of-options before URLs in clone, remote set-url, and ls-remote, and before refs in rev-parse. The checkout and reset calls are left alone, since covering those would need a floor of 2.43.1, released February 2024, and that’s recent enough to still be ahead of several supported distributions.

  1. Bundler, Cargo, CocoaPods, Composer, Conan, Go, Helm, Homebrew, Mix, Nix, npm, pip, pnpm, Poetry, Pub, SwiftPM, uv, vcpkg, Yarn. All checked at HEAD in July 2026. ↩

My first reaction was that some LLM had hallucinated it, but it’s documented in gitcli(7), it was added in git 2.24.0 in November 2019, and it exists because git had already used -- for something else.

sentences.en: I was reading through the fix for a package manager CVE last week and ran into a git flag I’d somehow never noticed: --end-of-options.
sentences.zh: 上周我正在阅读包管理器 CVE 的修复程序，并遇到了一个我从未注意到的 git 标志：--end-of-options。

sentences.en: My first reaction was that some LLM had hallucinated it, but it’s documented in gitcli(7), it was added in git 2.24.0 in November 2019, and it exists because git had already used -- for something else.
sentences.zh: 我的第一反应是一些 LLM 产生了幻觉，但它记录在 gitcli(7) 中，它于 2019 年 11 月添加到 git 2.24.0 中，它的存在是因为 git 已经用于其他用途。

blocks.html_en: In most Unix tools -- marks the end of option parsing, so rm -- -f removes a file called -f rather than passing the force flag. Git had repurposed -- early on to separate revisions from pathspecs, because git log foo on its own is ambiguous between a branch named foo and a file named foo and one of the two readings needed a marker: git log main -- README.md means commits on main touching that file. That left the revision position with no terminator, so if a script runs git log "$rev" and $rev starts with a dash, git parses it as an option.

sentences.en: In most Unix tools -- marks the end of option parsing, so rm -- -f removes a file called -f rather than passing the force flag.
sentences.zh: 在大多数 Unix 工具中 -- 标记选项解析的结束，因此 rm -- -f 删除名为 -f 的文件而不是传递强制标志。

sentences.en: Git had repurposed -- early on to separate revisions from pathspecs, because git log foo on its own is ambiguous between a branch named foo and a file named foo and one of the two readings needed a marker: git log main -- README.md means commits on main touching that file.
sentences.zh: Git 很早就重新调整了用途，将修订与路径规范分开，因为 git log foo 本身在名为 foo 的分支和名为 foo 的文件之间是不明确的，并且两个读数之一需要一个标记：git log main - README.md 意味着在 main 上提交触及该文件。

sentences.en: That left the revision position with no terminator, so if a script runs git log "$rev" and $rev starts with a dash, git parses it as an option.
sentences.zh: 这使得修订位置没有终止符，因此如果脚本运行 git log "$rev" 并且 $rev 以破折号开头，git 会将其解析为选项。

sentences.en: Putting -- before a URL in git clone -- "$url" works, because clone follows the POSIX convention.
sentences.zh: 在 git clone 中的 URL 之前放置“$url”是有效的，因为克隆遵循 POSIX 约定。

sentences.en: A trailing -- after a ref, as in git checkout "$ref" --, marks $ref as a revision rather than a filename but still lets it be read as an option first.
sentences.zh: ref 后面的尾随 --，如 git checkout "$ref" -- ，将 $ref 标记为修订版而不是文件名，但仍然允许它首先作为选项读取。

sentences.en: Passing an untrusted revision safely means writing git log --end-of-options "$rev" -- "$path", with both markers doing separate jobs.
sentences.zh: 安全地传递不受信任的修订意味着编写 git log --end-of-options "$rev" -- "$path"，两个标记执行单独的工作。

sentences.en: Mercurial accepts --config=alias.<subcmd>=!<shell> on any subcommand, which redefines the subcommand you’re running as an arbitrary shell script.
sentences.zh: Mercurial 在任何子命令上接受 --config=alias.<subcmd>=!<shell> ，这会将您正在运行的子命令重新定义为任意 shell 脚本。

sentences.en: ssh accepts -oProxyCommand=<cmd>.
sentences.zh: ssh 接受 -oProxyCommand=<cmd>。

sentences.en: These are documented features that become attack primitives when a wrapping program passes an untrusted string into the argument list.
sentences.zh: 当包装程序将不受信任的字符串传递到参数列表时，这些已记录的功能将成为攻击原语。

sentences.zh: 失败模式有自己的 CWE、CWE-88、参数注入，它与命令注入不同，因为不涉及 shell：包装程序构建一个 argv 数组并直接调用 exec，正如每个“不要使用 system()”指南所建议的那样，该数组完好无损地到达 git，然后 git 将其中一个参数解析为选项，因为它以破折号开头。

sentences.en: CVE-2019-13139 in docker build is a clean example: Go’s os/exec package, an argv array, no shell, and a git-context URL whose #ref:dir fragment reached git fetch origin <ref> as --upload-pack=<cmd>.
sentences.zh: docker build 中的 CVE-2019-13139 是一个干净的示例：Go 的 os/exec 包、argv 数组、无 shell 和 git-context URL，其 #ref:dir 片段以 --upload-pack=<cmd> 形式到达 git fetch origin <ref>。

blocks.html_en: The pattern was demonstrated across four version control systems on the same day in August 2017, when CVE-2017-1000117 (git), CVE-2017-1000116 (Mercurial), CVE-2017-9800 (Subversion), and CVE-2017-12836 (CVS) were disclosed together. Each passed a URL’s hostname to ssh as an argument, and a hostname starting with -oProxyCommand= became an ssh option. Phabricator’s post-mortem on the disclosure noted that of the three actively maintained tools, only Subversion actually added -- before the hostname in its fix; git and Mercurial validated the hostname format instead, partly because -- isn’t supported by every ssh implementation. The same write-up called the -- mechanism itself “unsafe by default”, since code without it looks correct and works fine right up until an argument starts with a dash.

sentences.en: The pattern was demonstrated across four version control systems on the same day in August 2017, when CVE-2017-1000117 (git), CVE-2017-1000116 (Mercurial), CVE-2017-9800 (Subversion), and CVE-2017-12836 (CVS) were disclosed together.
sentences.zh: 该模式于 2017 年 8 月的同一天在四个版本控制系统中得到了证明，当时 CVE-2017-1000117 (git)、CVE-2017-1000116 (Mercurial)、CVE-2017-9800 (Subversion) 和 CVE-2017-12836 (CVS) 一起被披露。

sentences.en: Each passed a URL’s hostname to ssh as an argument, and a hostname starting with -oProxyCommand= became an ssh option.
sentences.zh: 每个都将 URL 的主机名作为参数传递给 ssh，并且以 -oProxyCommand= 开头的主机名成为 ssh 选项。

sentences.en: Phabricator’s post-mortem on the disclosure noted that of the three actively maintained tools, only Subversion actually added -- before the hostname in its fix; git and Mercurial validated the hostname format instead, partly because -- isn’t supported by every ssh implementation.
sentences.zh: Phabricator 对披露的事后分析指出，在三个积极维护的工具中，只有 Subversion 实际上添加了——在其修复中的主机名之前； git 和 Mercurial 验证了主机名格式，部分原因是 -- 并非每个 ssh 实现都支持。

sentences.en: The same write-up called the -- mechanism itself “unsafe by default”, since code without it looks correct and works fine right up until an argument starts with a dash.
sentences.zh: 同一篇文章称 -- 机制本身“默认不安全”，因为没有它的代码看起来是正确的并且可以正常工作，直到参数以破折号开头。

blocks.html_en: Package managers

sentences.en: Package managers
sentences.zh: 包管理器

blocks.html_en: Package managers routinely take a git URL or ref as data and pass it to a subprocess: gem 'foo', git: '...' in a Gemfile, github:user/repo#ref in a package.json, and equivalents in pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, mix.exs, Package.swift, pubspec.yaml, conanfile.py, and go.mod. The URL and ref arrive in a manifest, a lockfile, or a transitive dependency’s metadata.

sentences.en: Package managers routinely take a git URL or ref as data and pass it to a subprocess: gem 'foo', git: '...'
sentences.zh: 包管理器通常将 git URL 或 ref 作为数据并将其传递给子进程：gem 'foo', git: '...'

sentences.en: in a Gemfile, github:user/repo#ref in a package.json, and equivalents in pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, mix.exs, Package.swift, pubspec.yaml, conanfile.py, and go.mod.
sentences.zh: 在 Gemfile 中，github:user/repo#ref 在 package.json 中，以及 pyproject.toml、Cargo.toml、mix.exs、Package.swift、pubspec.yaml、conanfile.py 和 go.mod 中的等效项。

sentences.en: The URL and ref arrive in a manifest, a lockfile, or a transitive dependency’s metadata.
sentences.zh: URL 和 ref 到达清单、锁定文件或传递依赖项的元数据中。

blocks.html_en: Of nineteen package managers I checked1, seventeen fork the git binary as their default or only path. The two that default to a library are Cargo, which uses libgit2 with an opt-in net.git-fetch-with-cli setting to fork instead, and Poetry, which switched to dulwich in 1.2.0 with a system-git-client setting to fall back. Nix uses libgit2 for reading local repositories but forks git for fetches, because libgit2 lacks git-credential helper support.

sentences.en: Of nineteen package managers I checked1, seventeen fork the git binary as their default or only path.
sentences.zh: 在我检查的 19 个包管理器中，有 17 个将 git 二进制文件分叉为默认或唯一路径。

sentences.en: The two that default to a library are Cargo, which uses libgit2 with an opt-in net.git-fetch-with-cli setting to fork instead, and Poetry, which switched to dulwich in 1.2.0 with a system-git-client setting to fall back.
sentences.zh: 默认使用库的两个是 Cargo，它使用 libgit2 并通过选择加入 net.git-fetch-with-cli 设置来 fork，而 Poetry 在 1.2.0 中切换到 dulwich，并通过 system-git-client 设置进行回退。

sentences.en: Nix uses libgit2 for reading local repositories but forks git for fetches, because libgit2 lacks git-credential helper support.
sentences.zh: Nix 使用 libgit2 读取本地存储库，但派生 git 进行提取，因为 libgit2 缺乏 git-credential helper 支持。

blocks.html_en: The published CVEs against package managers in this class include CVE-2021-43809 (Bundler), CVE-2021-29472 and CVE-2022-24828 (Composer), CVE-2022-36069 (Poetry), CVE-2023-5752 (pip), CVE-2022-21223 and CVE-2022-24440 (CocoaPods), and CVE-2025-68119 (Go). The Snyk research that produced several of the 2022 entries is written up here, and Sonar maintains a catalogue of the dangerous options per binary.

sentences.en: The published CVEs against package managers in this class include CVE-2021-43809 (Bundler), CVE-2021-29472 and CVE-2022-24828 (Composer), CVE-2022-36069 (Poetry), CVE-2023-5752 (pip), CVE-2022-21223 and CVE-2022-24440 (CocoaPods), and CVE-2025-68119 (Go).
sentences.zh: 针对此类中的包管理器的已发布 CVE 包括 CVE-2021-43809 (Bundler)、CVE-2021-29472 和 CVE-2022-24828 (Composer)、CVE-2022-36069 (Poetry)、CVE-2023-5752 (pip)、CVE-2022-21223 和CVE-2022-24440 (CocoaPods) 和 CVE-2025-68119 (Go)。

sentences.en: The Snyk research that produced several of the 2022 entries is written up here, and Sonar maintains a catalogue of the dangerous options per binary.
sentences.zh: 此处记录了 Snyk 研究生成的 2022 年多个条目，Sonar 维护了每个二进制文件的危险选项目录。

blocks.html_en: Of the seventeen that fork git, exactly one uses --end-of-options: Go’s cmd/go. It added -- before repository URLs in June 2019 as a general hardening pass. In January 2026 that turned out to be insufficient and --end-of-options was added across the board as the fix for CVE-2025-68119, along with HGPLAIN=+strictflags, which has restricted Mercurial’s early-option parsing since hg 4.4.2 in 2017. The commit message ends: “We should probably follow up with a more structured change to make it harder to accidentally re-introduce these issues in the future, but for now this addresses the issue at hand.”

sentences.en: Of the seventeen that fork git, exactly one uses --end-of-options: Go’s cmd/go.
sentences.zh: 在 17 个 fork git 中，只有一个使用 --end-of-options：Go 的 cmd/go。

sentences.en: It added -- before repository URLs in June 2019 as a general hardening pass.
sentences.zh: 它于 2019 年 6 月在存储库 URL 之前添加了 -- 作为一般强化过程。

sentences.en: In January 2026 that turned out to be insufficient and --end-of-options was added across the board as the fix for CVE-2025-68119, along with HGPLAIN=+strictflags, which has restricted Mercurial’s early-option parsing since hg 4.4.2 in 2017.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 1 月，事实证明这是不够的，全面添加了 --end-of-options 作为 CVE-2025-68119 的修复程序，以及 HGPLAIN=+strictflags，后者自 2017 年的 hg 4.4.2 以来限制了 Mercurial 的早期选项解析。

sentences.en: The commit message ends: “We should probably follow up with a more structured change to make it harder to accidentally re-introduce these issues in the future, but for now this addresses the issue at hand.”
sentences.zh: 提交消息的结尾是：“我们可能应该进行更结构化的更改，以使将来更难意外地重新引入这些问题，但目前这解决了当前的问题。”

blocks.html_en: Minimum git versions

sentences.en: Minimum git versions
sentences.zh: 最低 git 版本

blocks.html_en: The other package managers that guard the argument list at all use -- or a leading-dash check on the input, and looking at when each guard was added, most arrived as the fix for a reported vulnerability rather than in the original implementation. The -- before the URL in Bundler’s git clone is the CVE-2021-43809 patch. The leading-dash rejection in cocoapods-downloader landed in three commits over ten days in March 2022, matching the CVE-2022-21223 disclosure. Poetry’s guard arrived in September 2021 with a CVE assigned a year later, and the switch to dulwich followed six months after that. vcpkg is the exception I found where -- was present from the day git registry support was written.

sentences.en: The other package managers that guard the argument list at all use -- or a leading-dash check on the input, and looking at when each guard was added, most arrived as the fix for a reported vulnerability rather than in the original implementation.
sentences.zh: 其他保护参数列表的包管理器在所有使用中 - 或者对输入进行前导破折号检查，并查看每个保护的添加时间，大多数都是作为报告漏洞的修复而不是原始实现。

sentences.en: The -- before the URL in Bundler’s git clone is the CVE-2021-43809 patch.
sentences.zh: Bundler 的 git 克隆中的 URL 前面的 -- 是 CVE-2021-43809 补丁。

sentences.en: The leading-dash rejection in cocoapods-downloader landed in three commits over ten days in March 2022, matching the CVE-2022-21223 disclosure.
sentences.zh: cocoapods-downloader 中的leading-dash 拒绝在 2022 年 3 月的十天内提交了 3 次，与 CVE-2022-21223 的披露相符。

sentences.en: Poetry’s guard arrived in September 2021 with a CVE assigned a year later, and the switch to dulwich followed six months after that.
sentences.zh: Poetry 的警卫于 2021 年 9 月抵达，一年后分配了 CVE，六个月后转投德威。

sentences.en: vcpkg is the exception I found where -- was present from the day git registry support was written.
sentences.zh: vcpkg 是我发现的例外，其中 -- 从编写 git 注册表支持之日起就存在。

blocks.html_en: Composer’s advisory for CVE-2022-24828 explains why almost none of these tools use --end-of-options: it names the flag as the correct fix and then says Composer supports git versions that predate it, so the patch rejects leading-dash branch names instead. vcpkg’s git integration has a comment stating a floor of git 2.7.4. Homebrew’s HOMEBREW_MINIMUM_GIT_VERSION is 2.7.0 on Linux, set in 2018.

sentences.en: Composer’s advisory for CVE-2022-24828 explains why almost none of these tools use --end-of-options: it names the flag as the correct fix and then says Composer supports git versions that predate it, so the patch rejects leading-dash branch names instead.
sentences.zh: Composer 针对 CVE-2022-24828 的公告解释了为什么这些工具几乎没有使用 --end-of-options：它将标志命名为正确的修复，然后表示 Composer 支持早于它的 git 版本，因此该补丁会拒绝前导破折号分支名称。

sentences.en: vcpkg’s git integration has a comment stating a floor of git 2.7.4.
sentences.zh: vcpkg 的 git 集成有一条评论指出 git 2.7.4 的底层。

sentences.en: Homebrew’s HOMEBREW_MINIMUM_GIT_VERSION is 2.7.0 on Linux, set in 2018.
sentences.zh: Linux 上 Homebrew 的 HOMEBREW_MINIMUM_GIT_VERSION 是 2.7.0，设置于 2018 年。

blocks.html_en: Amazon Linux 2, which packaged git 2.14.3, reached end of life last month, so the distributions those floors track are only now ageing out. Ubuntu 18.04 with git 2.17.0 is in extended support until 2028. Ubuntu 20.04, in extended support until 2030, packages 2.25.1, which is new enough to accept --end-of-options on git fetch but old enough to reject it on git rev-parse. Relying on the flag means raising the minimum git to 2.24.0 for most subcommands, 2.30.0 for rev-parse, or 2.43.1 for checkout and reset, and losing anyone still on the distribution-packaged git.

sentences.en: Amazon Linux 2, which packaged git 2.14.3, reached end of life last month, so the distributions those floors track are only now ageing out.
sentences.zh: 打包了 git 2.14.3 的 Amazon Linux 2 已于上个月终止生命周期，因此这些楼层跟踪的发行版现在才刚刚过时。

sentences.en: Ubuntu 18.04 with git 2.17.0 is in extended support until 2028.
sentences.zh: 带有 git 2.17.0 的 Ubuntu 18.04 的延长支持期到 2028 年。

sentences.en: Ubuntu 20.04, in extended support until 2030, packages 2.25.1, which is new enough to accept --end-of-options on git fetch but old enough to reject it on git rev-parse.
sentences.zh: Ubuntu 20.04，扩展支持到 2030 年，软件包 2.25.1，新到足以在 git fetch 上接受 --end-of-options，但又老到足以在 git rev-parse 上拒绝它。

sentences.en: Relying on the flag means raising the minimum git to 2.24.0 for most subcommands, 2.30.0 for rev-parse, or 2.43.1 for checkout and reset, and losing anyone still on the distribution-packaged git.
sentences.zh: 依赖该标志意味着将大多数子命令的最小 git 提高到 2.24.0，对于 rev-parse 提高到 2.30.0，或者对于签出和重置提高到 2.43.1，并且失去仍在发行版打包的 git 上的任何人。

blocks.html_en: Git libraries

sentences.en: Git libraries
sentences.zh: Git 库

blocks.html_en: libgit2, gitoxide, go-git, JGit, and dulwich all implement enough of the git wire protocol to clone and fetch in-process, with no argv boundary and so no argument list to inject into. Jujutsu uses gitoxide for its git interop and has no published CVEs in the argument-injection class; its two advisories to date are a path traversal and a missing SHA-1 collision check inherited from the library. go-git has one, CVE-2025-21613, and it’s specifically on the file:// transport, the one code path in go-git that spawns the git binary.

sentences.en: libgit2, gitoxide, go-git, JGit, and dulwich all implement enough of the git wire protocol to clone and fetch in-process, with no argv boundary and so no argument list to inject into.
sentences.zh: libgit2、giticide、go-git、JGit 和 dulwich 都实现了足够的 gitwire 协议来克隆和获取进程内的数据，没有 argv 边界，因此没有要注入的参数列表。

sentences.en: Jujutsu uses gitoxide for its git interop and has no published CVEs in the argument-injection class; its two advisories to date are a path traversal and a missing SHA-1 collision check inherited from the library.
sentences.zh: Jujutsu 使用 gitoxy 进行 git 互操作，并且在参数注入类中没有发布的 CVE；迄今为止，它的两个建议是路径遍历和从库继承的缺少 SHA-1 冲突检查。

sentences.en: go-git has one, CVE-2025-21613, and it’s specifically on the file:// transport, the one code path in go-git that spawns the git binary.
sentences.zh: go-git 有一个 CVE-2025-21613，它专门位于 file:// 传输上，这是 go-git 中生成 git 二进制文件的一个代码路径。

blocks.html_en: I noted in the package manager CWEs post that this trades one problem for another, since a bundled git implementation has to track every checkout-safety fix upstream git ships, and libgit2 and JGit have both had rounds of those. That’s a real cost, but it’s a stream of specific patches to apply rather than a check that has to be remembered at every call site forever.

sentences.en: I noted in the package manager CWEs post that this trades one problem for another, since a bundled git implementation has to track every checkout-safety fix upstream git ships, and libgit2 and JGit have both had rounds of those.
sentences.zh: 我在包管理器 CWE 帖子中指出，这将一个问题换成另一个问题，因为捆绑的 git 实现必须跟踪上游 git 船舶的每个签出安全修复，而 libgit2 和 JGit 都有这些问题。

sentences.en: That’s a real cost, but it’s a stream of specific patches to apply rather than a check that has to be remembered at every call site forever.
sentences.zh: 这是一个真正的成本，但它是要应用的一系列特定补丁，而不是必须在每个调用站点永远记住的检查。

blocks.html_en: Writing this prompted me to open a PR against Homebrew raising its minimum git to 2.30.0 and adding --end-of-options before URLs in clone, remote set-url, and ls-remote, and before refs in rev-parse. The checkout and reset calls are left alone, since covering those would need a floor of 2.43.1, released February 2024, and that’s recent enough to still be ahead of several supported distributions.

sentences.en: Writing this prompted me to open a PR against Homebrew raising its minimum git to 2.30.0 and adding --end-of-options before URLs in clone, remote set-url, and ls-remote, and before refs in rev-parse.
sentences.zh: 写这篇文章促使我针对 Homebrew 提出 PR，将其最小 git 提高到 2.30.0，并在克隆、远程 set-url 和 ls-remote 中的 URL 之前以及 rev-parse 中的引用之前添加 --end-of-options。

sentences.en: The checkout and reset calls are left alone, since covering those would need a floor of 2.43.1, released February 2024, and that’s recent enough to still be ahead of several supported distributions.
sentences.zh: 结帐和重置调用将被保留，因为覆盖这些调用需要 2024 年 2 月发布的 2.43.1 的下限，而且这个版本足够新，仍然领先于几个受支持的发行版。

blocks.html_en: • Bundler, Cargo, CocoaPods, Composer, Conan, Go, Helm, Homebrew, Mix, Nix, npm, pip, pnpm, Poetry, Pub, SwiftPM, uv, vcpkg, Yarn. All checked at HEAD in July 2026. ↩

sentences.en: Bundler, Cargo, CocoaPods, Composer, Conan, Go, Helm, Homebrew, Mix, Nix, npm, pip, pnpm, Poetry, Pub, SwiftPM, uv, vcpkg, Yarn.
sentences.zh: Bundler、Cargo、CocoaPods、Composer、Conan、Go、Helm、Homebrew、Mix、Nix、npm、pip、pnpm、Poetry、Pub、SwiftPM、uv、vcpkg、Yarn。

sentences.en: All checked at HEAD in July 2026. ↩
sentences.zh: 全部于 2026 年 7 月在 HEAD 检查。 ↩

blocks.html_en: Bundler, Cargo, CocoaPods, Composer, Conan, Go, Helm, Homebrew, Mix, Nix, npm, pip, pnpm, Poetry, Pub, SwiftPM, uv, vcpkg, Yarn. All checked at HEAD in July 2026. ↩

sentences.en: Bundler, Cargo, CocoaPods, Composer, Conan, Go, Helm, Homebrew, Mix, Nix, npm, pip, pnpm, Poetry, Pub, SwiftPM, uv, vcpkg, Yarn.
sentences.zh: Bundler、Cargo、CocoaPods、Composer、Conan、Go、Helm、Homebrew、Mix、Nix、npm、pip、pnpm、Poetry、Pub、SwiftPM、uv、vcpkg、Yarn。

sentences.en: All checked at HEAD in July 2026. ↩
sentences.zh: 全部于 2026 年 7 月在 HEAD 检查。 ↩

content_html_zh:
上周我正在阅读包管理器 CVE 的修复程序，并遇到了一个我从未注意到的 git 标志：--end-of-options。 我的第一反应是一些 LLM 产生了幻觉，但它记录在 gitcli(7) 中，它于 2019 年 11 月添加到 git 2.24.0 中，它的存在是因为 git 已经用于其他用途。

在大多数 Unix 工具中 -- 标记选项解析的结束，因此 rm -- -f 删除名为 -f 的文件而不是传递强制标志。 Git 很早就重新调整了用途，将修订与路径规范分开，因为 git log foo 本身在名为 foo 的分支和名为 foo 的文件之间是不明确的，并且两个读数之一需要一个标记：git log main - README.md 意味着在 main 上提交触及该文件。 这使得修订位置没有终止符，因此如果脚本运行 git log "$rev" 并且 $rev 以破折号开头，git 会将其解析为选项。

来自引入 --end-of-options 的提交：

但这对于修订解析器不起作用，因为 -- 在那里已经有意义：它将修订与路径规范分开。 因此，我们需要一些其他标记来区分选项和修订。

-- 和 --end-of-options 在 git 中是不同的东西，将它们视为可互换的是我现在在多个地方看到的错误。 在 git clone 中的 URL 之前放置“$url”是有效的，因为克隆遵循 POSIX 约定。 ref 后面的尾随 --，如 git checkout "$ref" -- ，将 $ref 标记为修订版而不是文件名，但仍然允许它首先作为选项读取。 安全地传递不受信任的修订意味着编写 git log --end-of-options "$rev" -- "$path"，两个标记执行单独的工作。

对新标志的支持是每个子命令而不是一次性全部实现： git rev-parse 只在 2.30.0 中获得它，即初始版本一年后，因为它有自己的手动参数解析器，并且 git checkout 和 git Reset 拒绝了它，直到 2024 年 2 月的 2.43.1 为止，因为它们解析 -- 自己和初始实现在参数列表中留下了 --end-of-options ，而它们的解析器拒绝了它。

参数注入

Git、hg 和 ssh 所有选项的记录目的都是运行调用者指定的命令。 git clone 接受 --upload-pack=<cmd> 来指定服务器端二进制文件，任何 git 调用都接受 -c core.sshCommand=<cmd> 来覆盖它的连接方式。 Mercurial 在任何子命令上接受 --config=alias.<subcmd>=!<shell> ，这会将您正在运行的子命令重新定义为任意 shell 脚本。 ssh 接受 -oProxyCommand=<cmd>。 当包装程序将不受信任的字符串传递到参数列表时，这些已记录的功能将成为攻击原语。

失败模式有自己的 CWE、CWE-88、参数注入，它与命令注入不同，因为不涉及 shell：包装程序构建一个 argv 数组并直接调用 exec，正如每个“不要使用 system()”指南所建议的那样，该数组完好无损地到达 git，然后 git 将其中一个参数解析为选项，因为它以破折号开头。 docker build 中的 CVE-2019-13139 是一个干净的示例：Go 的 os/exec 包、argv 数组、无 shell 和 git-context URL，其 #ref:dir 片段以 --upload-pack=<cmd> 形式到达 git fetch origin <ref>。

该模式于 2017 年 8 月的同一天在四个版本控制系统中得到了证明，当时 CVE-2017-1000117 (git)、CVE-2017-1000116 (Mercurial)、CVE-2017-9800 (Subversion) 和 CVE-2017-12836 (CVS) 一起被披露。 每个都将 URL 的主机名作为参数传递给 ssh，并且以 -oProxyCommand= 开头的主机名成为 ssh 选项。 Phabricator 对披露的事后分析指出，在三个积极维护的工具中，只有 Subversion 实际上添加了——在其修复中的主机名之前； git 和 Mercurial 验证了主机名格式，部分原因是 -- 并非每个 ssh 实现都支持。 同一篇文章称 -- 机制本身“默认不安全”，因为没有它的代码看起来是正确的并且可以正常工作，直到参数以破折号开头。

包管理器

包管理器通常将 git URL 或 ref 作为数据并将其传递给子进程：gem 'foo', git: '...' 在 Gemfile 中，github:user/repo#ref 在 package.json 中，以及 pyproject.toml、Cargo.toml、mix.exs、Package.swift、pubspec.yaml、conanfile.py 和 go.mod 中的等效项。 URL 和 ref 到达清单、锁定文件或传递依赖项的元数据中。

在我检查的 19 个包管理器中，有 17 个将 git 二进制文件分叉为默认或唯一路径。 默认使用库的两个是 Cargo，它使用 libgit2 并通过选择加入 net.git-fetch-with-cli 设置来 fork，而 Poetry 在 1.2.0 中切换到 dulwich，并通过 system-git-client 设置进行回退。 Nix 使用 libgit2 读取本地存储库，但派生 git 进行提取，因为 libgit2 缺乏 git-credential helper 支持。

针对此类中的包管理器的已发布 CVE 包括 CVE-2021-43809 (Bundler)、CVE-2021-29472 和 CVE-2022-24828 (Composer)、CVE-2022-36069 (Poetry)、CVE-2023-5752 (pip)、CVE-2022-21223 和CVE-2022-24440 (CocoaPods) 和 CVE-2025-68119 (Go)。 此处记录了 Snyk 研究生成的 2022 年多个条目，Sonar 维护了每个二进制文件的危险选项目录。

在 17 个 fork git 中，只有一个使用 --end-of-options：Go 的 cmd/go。 它于 2019 年 6 月在存储库 URL 之前添加了 -- 作为一般强化过程。 2026 年 1 月，事实证明这是不够的，全面添加了 --end-of-options 作为 CVE-2025-68119 的修复程序，以及 HGPLAIN=+strictflags，后者自 2017 年的 hg 4.4.2 以来限制了 Mercurial 的早期选项解析。 提交消息的结尾是：“我们可能应该进行更结构化的更改，以使将来更难意外地重新引入这些问题，但目前这解决了当前的问题。”

最低 git 版本

其他保护参数列表的包管理器在所有使用中 - 或者对输入进行前导破折号检查，并查看每个保护的添加时间，大多数都是作为报告漏洞的修复而不是原始实现。 Bundler 的 git 克隆中的 URL 前面的 -- 是 CVE-2021-43809 补丁。 cocoapods-downloader 中的leading-dash 拒绝在 2022 年 3 月的十天内提交了 3 次，与 CVE-2022-21223 的披露相符。 Poetry 的警卫于 2021 年 9 月抵达，一年后分配了 CVE，六个月后转投德威。 vcpkg 是我发现的例外，其中 -- 从编写 git 注册表支持之日起就存在。

Composer 针对 CVE-2022-24828 的公告解释了为什么这些工具几乎没有使用 --end-of-options：它将标志命名为正确的修复，然后表示 Composer 支持早于它的 git 版本，因此该补丁会拒绝前导破折号分支名称。 vcpkg 的 git 集成有一条评论指出 git 2.7.4 的底层。 Linux 上 Homebrew 的 HOMEBREW_MINIMUM_GIT_VERSION 是 2.7.0，设置于 2018 年。

打包了 git 2.14.3 的 Amazon Linux 2 已于上个月终止生命周期，因此这些楼层跟踪的发行版现在才刚刚过时。 带有 git 2.17.0 的 Ubuntu 18.04 的延长支持期到 2028 年。 Ubuntu 20.04，扩展支持到 2030 年，软件包 2.25.1，新到足以在 git fetch 上接受 --end-of-options，但又老到足以在 git rev-parse 上拒绝它。 依赖该标志意味着将大多数子命令的最小 git 提高到 2.24.0，对于 rev-parse 提高到 2.30.0，或者对于签出和重置提高到 2.43.1，并且失去仍在发行版打包的 git 上的任何人。

Git 库

libgit2、giticide、go-git、JGit 和 dulwich 都实现了足够的 gitwire 协议来克隆和获取进程内的数据，没有 argv 边界，因此没有要注入的参数列表。 Jujutsu 使用 gitoxy 进行 git 互操作，并且在参数注入类中没有发布的 CVE；迄今为止，它的两个建议是路径遍历和从库继承的缺少 SHA-1 冲突检查。 go-git 有一个 CVE-2025-21613，它专门位于 file:// 传输上，这是 go-git 中生成 git 二进制文件的一个代码路径。

我在包管理器 CWE 帖子中指出，这将一个问题换成另一个问题，因为捆绑的 git 实现必须跟踪上游 git 船舶的每个签出安全修复，而 libgit2 和 JGit 都有这些问题。 这是一个真正的成本，但它是要应用的一系列特定补丁，而不是必须在每个调用站点永远记住的检查。

写这篇文章促使我针对 Homebrew 提出 PR，将其最小 git 提高到 2.30.0，并在克隆、远程 set-url 和 ls-remote 中的 URL 之前以及 rev-parse 中的引用之前添加 --end-of-options。 结帐和重置调用将被保留，因为覆盖这些调用需要 2024 年 2 月发布的 2.43.1 的下限，而且这个版本足够新，仍然领先于几个受支持的发行版。

  1. Bundler、Cargo、CocoaPods、Composer、Conan、Go、Helm、Homebrew、Mix、Nix、npm、pip、pnpm、Poetry、Pub、SwiftPM、uv、vcpkg、Yarn。 全部于 2026 年 7 月在 HEAD 检查。 ↩
